full_transcript: Hello Friends, Welcome Back In this video, we will try to know what are the inheritance and how to achieve it For which I have written a simple program which is right in front of your screen So, here I have written a class called Human, another class called Student Although, another class is the main method which is the Java program Apart from that, we have two classes, one is Human and one is Student If I ask you a question, is there any link or relationship between student class and human class? The answer should be No For now, these two classes are independent, they don't have any relationship with each other This is an independent class and this is an independent class, there is no relationship Where I have made a simple property in Human class, feel, instance, variable, property, age, and I have written a behavior, a method called sleep I have initialized the age and I am printing a simple message, Human needs a good sleep And the value in the age, I am just printing it in a console So, basically, there is a basic method and a property in the Human class And there is nothing in the Student class And these two classes are different from each other, there is no link and that is an independent class So, what will I do here? I will make an object of a student Okay, I have made an object of a student, ST equals to New Student After making an object of a student, I will do ST.Sleep Meaning, I am calling the Sleep method here and I am getting an error here That the method Sleep is undefined First of all, you are making an object of a student here, okay, we have made an object of a student After that, you are using its reference to call a Sleep method Now, tell me, there is no Sleep method in the Student class That is why we are getting an error here That you are calling the Sleep method, take the reference of the Student object Now, there is no Sleep method there, that is why the compiler is giving us an error here Where is the Sleep method? It is here, it is in the Human class So, here a message goes that this is a different class or there is no relationship between these two classes And there is no link Now, what will I do? I will establish a relationship between these two classes I will make a relationship and a link, okay Where you see what exactly will happen So, I will use a Keyword here, with which whatever error is coming here, that error will go away That Keyword is Extents, the Keyword of Extents And I will write the name of the class, Human So, the moment you say Extents Human, you see that there is an error here, the compiler is happy So, what happened in Extents Keyword? Extents Keyword is used to establish a relationship between two classes It means, a relationship is formed, in which we say in the simple terms, is our relationship Is our relationship, means Student is a Human Which is called Base class, drive class, super class, sub class, parent class, child class So, basically what is happening here? The moment you say Extents Human, in the Human class, whatever properties, methods were, everything has been inherited in this class That means, first this class and this class were Human and Student Independent classes Now, they have been linked with each other and a relationship has been formed between the two classes And this concept is called Inheritance The moment you say Extents, whatever is happening in this Human class, properties, behavior This class is inherited As a developer, you won't be able to see age and sleep methods from your eyes But this is here, because you don't see any error here Earlier there was an error, now there is no error Now there is no error, this means that the sleep method is present in the Student class If the sleep method was not present, then there would be an error, like before we were coming, now there is an error This is an indication that there is a sleep method here, I will execute it here once before moving forward You can see here, Human needs a good sleep, 18 Means this output is coming to you, and where is this output, this is the sleep method body, it is in the Human class Did you make the Human class object here? Not at all, we made the Student class object, if this is not in the Student class, then how is the output coming? This is proved here that the sleep method is being inherited in the Student class And how is it happening? With the help of Extent Keyword The moment you say Extent and whatever class name you write here, the properties of that class and methods will come to this class So you wrote the code here, you are reusing the same code here, you didn't write it here But as you made the object and called it, it is being executed, that means that sleep is also here This means that whatever you wrote here, you are reusing the same code here So this is code reusability, you wrote it once, you are reusing the same code here So this concept is inheritance I will tell you a simple definition, it is a process where one class acquiring properties and behavior of another class This class is acquiring the properties and behavior of this class, and it will come here This creates a relationship with which we know from the name of Easy Relationship See, Student is a Human, that is why we call it Easy Relationship, which we call Parent Shell So from the place where you are acquiring properties, we usually call that class Super Class Super Class, or we can call it Base Class, or we can call it Parent Class So all this means that Parent Class, Base Class, Super Class, everyone has the same meaning So the class you are acquiring properties from, now see here it is called Extent Human So this class, we can call it Super Class, Base Class, Parent Class And the class in which you are acquiring properties, in the Student Class, you are acquiring them in the Student Class So then you can call it Student Class, or we can call it Child Class, or we can call it Sub Class, or we can call it Derived Class Super Class, Sub Class, Derived Class, or Child Class, all this is the same The class that is acquiring other things, we call it Child Class, or we can call it Sub Class Or we can also call it Derived Class The class you are acquiring, it is our Parent Class, or Base Class, or Super Class You are going to understand a lot about this, this is not the last video of Inheritance, this is the first video of Inheritance Where you understood how to relate two classes, and how to achieve Inheritance by using Extent Keyword Before we move ahead in this topic, let's see its definitions Of course without any doubt, it is one of the pillars of object oriented If you call yourself a Java Developer, because Java is an object oriented programming language It is very important for you to know about Inheritance It always speaks about code reusability, where you are reusing the code So of course using Inheritance, productivity of the code can be improved If you use Inheritance, lines of the code can be reduced to optimize the application As we understand the concept of these UPS, we will also make a small application Where you will understand how to use this concept to make our code more efficient and optimized And of course, how to achieve Inheritance in Java, using the Extent Keyword This will help you to understand some more things about Inheritance in the coming videos

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Hello Friends, Welcome Back

chunks.text: In this video, we will try to know what are the inheritance and how to achieve it

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: For which I have written a simple program which is right in front of your screen

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So, here I have written a class called Human, another class called Student

chunks.text: Although, another class is the main method which is the Java program

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Apart from that, we have two classes, one is Human and one is Student

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: If I ask you a question, is there any link or relationship between student class and human class?

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: The answer should be No

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: For now, these two classes are independent, they don't have any relationship with each other

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: This is an independent class and this is an independent class, there is no relationship

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So, what will I do here? I will make an object of a student

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Now, there is no Sleep method there, that is why the compiler is giving us an error here

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Where is the Sleep method? It is here, it is in the Human class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So, here a message goes that this is a different class or there is no relationship between these two classes

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: And there is no link

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Now, what will I do? I will establish a relationship between these two classes

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: I will make a relationship and a link, okay

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Where you see what exactly will happen

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So, I will use a Keyword here, with which whatever error is coming here, that error will go away

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: And I will write the name of the class, Human

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So, the moment you say Extents Human, you see that there is an error here, the compiler is happy

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So, what happened in Extents Keyword?

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Extents Keyword is used to establish a relationship between two classes

chunks.text: It means, a relationship is formed, in which we say in the simple terms, is our relationship

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Is our relationship, means Student is a Human

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Which is called Base class, drive class, super class, sub class, parent class, child class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So, basically what is happening here?

chunks.text: The moment you say Extents Human, in the Human class, whatever properties, methods were, everything has been inherited in this class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Now, they have been linked with each other and a relationship has been formed between the two classes

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: And this concept is called Inheritance

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: The moment you say Extents, whatever is happening in this Human class, properties, behavior

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: This class is inherited

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: As a developer, you won't be able to see age and sleep methods from your eyes

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: But this is here, because you don't see any error here

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Earlier there was an error, now there is no error

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Now there is no error, this means that the sleep method is present in the Student class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: If the sleep method was not present, then there would be an error, like before we were coming, now there is an error

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: This is an indication that there is a sleep method here, I will execute it here once before moving forward

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: You can see here, Human needs a good sleep, 18

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Means this output is coming to you, and where is this output, this is the sleep method body, it is in the Human class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Did you make the Human class object here?

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Not at all, we made the Student class object, if this is not in the Student class, then how is the output coming?

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: This is proved here that the sleep method is being inherited in the Student class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: And how is it happening?

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: With the help of Extent Keyword

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: The moment you say Extent and whatever class name you write here, the properties of that class and methods will come to this class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So you wrote the code here, you are reusing the same code here, you didn't write it here

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: But as you made the object and called it, it is being executed, that means that sleep is also here

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: This means that whatever you wrote here, you are reusing the same code here

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So this is code reusability, you wrote it once, you are reusing the same code here

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So this concept is inheritance

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: I will tell you a simple definition, it is a process where one class acquiring properties and behavior of another class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: This class is acquiring the properties and behavior of this class, and it will come here

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: This creates a relationship with which we know from the name of Easy Relationship

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: See, Student is a Human, that is why we call it Easy Relationship, which we call Parent Shell

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So from the place where you are acquiring properties, we usually call that class Super Class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Super Class, or we can call it Base Class, or we can call it Parent Class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So all this means that Parent Class, Base Class, Super Class, everyone has the same meaning

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So the class you are acquiring properties from, now see here it is called Extent Human

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So this class, we can call it Super Class, Base Class, Parent Class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: And the class in which you are acquiring properties, in the Student Class, you are acquiring them in the Student Class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So then you can call it Student Class, or we can call it Child Class, or we can call it Sub Class, or we can call it Derived Class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Super Class, Sub Class, Derived Class, or Child Class, all this is the same

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: The class that is acquiring other things, we call it Child Class, or we can call it Sub Class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Or we can also call it Derived Class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: The class you are acquiring, it is our Parent Class, or Base Class, or Super Class

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: You are going to understand a lot about this, this is not the last video of Inheritance, this is the first video of Inheritance

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Where you understood how to relate two classes, and how to achieve Inheritance by using Extent Keyword

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Before we move ahead in this topic, let's see its definitions

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: Of course without any doubt, it is one of the pillars of object oriented

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: If you call yourself a Java Developer, because Java is an object oriented programming language

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: It is very important for you to know about Inheritance

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: It always speaks about code reusability, where you are reusing the code

chunks.title: Inheritance Introduction
chunks.text: So of course using Inheritance, productivity of the code can be improved
